prs.title: changed the name to extraction based on the reference repo

fix(ext/node): emit response and end events on http2 client streams
prs.body:
<!--
IMPORTANT: If you used AI tools (e.g. Copilot, ChatGPT, Claude, Cursor, etc.)
to help write this PR, you MUST disclose it in the PR description. PRs will be
rejected if there is suspicion of undisclosed AI usage.

Before submitting a PR, please read https://docs.deno.com/runtime/manual/references/contributing

1. Give the PR a descriptive title.

  Examples of good title:
    - fix(ext/net): fix race condition in TCP listener
    - docs(runtime): update permissions API docstrings
    - feat(cli): add --env-file flag to `deno run`
    - refactor(ext/node): simplify Buffer.from() implementation
    - test(ext/fs): add spec tests for symlink resolution

  Examples of bad title:
    - fix #7123
    - update docs
    - fix bugs

2. Ensure there is a related issue and it is referenced in the PR text.
3. Ensure there are tests that cover the changes.
4. Ensure `./x fmt` passes without changing files.
5. Ensure `./x lint` passes.
6. If you used AI tools to help write this PR, you MUST disclose it below.
   PRs will be rejected if there is suspicion of undisclosed AI usage.
7. Open as a draft PR if your work is still in progress. The CI won't run
   all steps, but you can add '[ci]' to a commit message to force it to.
8. If you would like to run the benchmarks on the CI, add the 'ci-bench' label.
-->
Fixes #32937

## Changes

- `onStreamClose`: removed the `NGHTTP2_SESSION_SERVER` guard so client
  streams also auto-resume when the stream closes, allowing `response`
  and `end` events to fire correctly
- `scheduleSendPending`: restored `queueMicrotask` deferral to avoid
  re-entrancy into nghttp2 callbacks

## Root cause

When a server closes an HTTP/2 stream, `onStreamClose()` only
auto-resumed the readable side for server sessions. Client sessions
fell into the `else` branch (`stream.read(0)`), which is a no-op when
the stream is still pending — causing `response` and `end` to never
emit and the process to hang indefinitely.

## Testing

Added a test in `tests/unit_node/http2_test.ts` that reproduces the
exact scenario from the issue — a client connecting, sending a request,
and asserting both `response` and `end` events fire.

## AI disclosure

This PR was developed with assistance from Claude (Anthropic).

prs.title: fix(lang-ansi): Fallback for missing theme ANSI colors
prs.body:
<!-- DO NOT IGNORE THE TEMPLATE!

Thank you for contributing!

Before submitting the PR, please make sure you do the following:

- Read the [Contributing Guide](https://github.com/shikijs/shiki/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md).
- Check that there isn't already a PR that solves the problem the same way to avoid creating a duplicate.
- Provide a description in this PR that addresses **what** the PR is solving, or reference the issue that it solves (e.g. `fixes #123`).
- Ideally, include relevant tests that fail without this PR but pass with it.

-->

### Description

This PR fixes an issue where ANSI language highlighting (`lang: ansi`) would fail on themes that do not provide their own `terminal.ansi*` colors (as seen with `one-light`).

The original code would attempt to read `theme.colors` for the ANSI palette. If a theme didn't define these colors, `undefined` was passed to the `createColorPalette` function, causing an error.

This change introduces a `defaultAnsiColors` map, which provides a standard VSCode-compatible set of fallback colors. The `tokenizeAnsiWithTheme` function now checks for a theme color and uses a color from this default map if one isn't found, ensuring the ANSI tokenizer always receives valid color values.

### Linked Issues

Fixes #1059

### Additional context

The `defaultAnsiColors` map is based on the standard VSCode fallbacks. This ensures consistent behavior for themes that don't specify their own terminal colors, resolving the bug while maintaining correctness for themes that do.

prs.title: Added rat in maze implementation in Python
prs.body: Implemented Rat in a Maze pathfinding algorithm in Python with DFS backtracking, BFS shortest path, and path counting variants. Includes coordinate tracking, path visualization, comprehensive test cases with 5 different maze scenarios, and performance benchmarks.

prs.title: Added login and signup page Chanakya
prs.body: Implemented the login page template with email and password fields, "Remember me" option, and "Forgot Password" link. Included navigation to the signup page for new users. linked to pr #76

prs.title: Feature/lps
prs.body: Implemented Longest Palindromic Subsequence (LPS) in Python with memoization, tabulation, space-optimized, and LCS-based variants. Includes palindrome string reconstruction, min insertions/deletions problems, comprehensive test cases, and performance benchmarks.

prs.title: Feature/0 1 knapsack
prs.body: Implemented 0/1 Knapsack problem in Python with memoization, tabulation, and O(W) space-optimized variants. Includes item selection tracking, fractional and unbounded variants, comprehensive test cases, and performance benchmarks.

prs.title: Added Frog jump min cost implementation in python
prs.body: Implemented Frog Jump (minimum cost) algorithm in Python with memoization, tabulation, and O(1) space-optimized variants. Includes path reconstruction, k-steps extension, comprehensive test cases, and performance benchmarks.

prs.title: feat: Added story from Chanakya
prs.body:
# ✨ Open Source Learning Stories – Pull Request Template

Thank you for contributing to **Open Source Learning Stories**! 🎉
Please ensure your PR follows the guidelines below to help us review it quickly.

---

## ✅ Author Checklist

- [x] I have read the [CONTRIBUTING.md](../CONTRIBUTING.md) guide.
- [x] My story is added inside the **`stories/`** folder.
- [x] The file is named correctly: **`<your-github-username>.md`**. (We recommend using your GitHub username for file naming consistency).
- [x] I used the proper story format (Name, Date, Tools, Experience, Advice).
- [x] I did not edit or delete someone else’s story.
- [x] My contribution is genuine and respectful.

---

## 📖 Description of Changes

Please provide a clear and brief description of your contribution.

(e.g., “Added my personal open-source journey story,” “Fixed a typo in the Java section of the README,” etc.)

---

## 🔗 Related Issue (Optional)

If this Pull Request resolves an existing issue, please link it here.

* Closes # (issue number)

---

## 🖼️ Screenshots / Preview (Optional)

If your contribution involves significant text changes or documentation fixes, you can provide a preview here.

---

🎉 **Tip:** Thank you again for being a part of this project! We appreciate your story!

prs.title: fix: resolved the issue video display on screen (fixed #22)
prs.body: ## 📋 Description This component provides a user interface for testing and confirming the functionality of a user's camera and microphone before a web call. It uses the Web MediaDevices API to display a live video feed, indicate connection status, and allow the user to toggle their video and audio tracks. ## 🔗 Related Issue Fixes #22 ## 🎯 Type of Change Please delete options that are not relevant. - [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue) ## 🧪 Testing Please describe the tests that you ran to verify your changes. **Test Configuration:** - Browser(s): [e.g. Chrome 96, Firefox 94] - OS: [e.g. Windows 10, macOS, Ubuntu] - Node.js version: [e.g. 18.17.0] **Manual Testing Checklist:** - [x] Video calls work correctly **(Verified video stream displays after 'Start Test' button is pressed)** - [x] Audio controls function properly **(Checked mute/unmute)** - [x] Camera controls function properly **(Checked video toggle)** - [ ] Room creation and joining works **(N/A for this component)** - [ ] Mobile responsive design verified - [x] Error handling works as expected **(Tested denial of permission)** - [ ] Performance is acceptable ## 📷 Screenshots (if applicable) Add screenshots of any UI changes or new features. **Before:** **After:** ## 📋 Checklist Please check all that apply: **Code Quality:** - [ x] My code follows the style guidelines of this project - [ x] I have performed a self-review of my own code - [ x] I have commented my code, particularly in hard-to-understand areas - [ ] I have made corresponding changes to the documentation - [ x] My changes generate no new warnings or errors - [ ] I have added tests that prove my fix is effective or that my feature works **Testing:** - [ ] New and existing unit tests pass locally with my changes - [ ] I have tested this on multiple browsers - [ ] I have tested this on mobile devices (if applicable) - [ x] Camera/microphone functionality tested (if applicable) **Documentation:** - [ ] I have updated the README.md (if needed) - [ ] I have updated the CHANGELOG.md - [ ] I have added/updated TypeScript types (if applicable) ## 🔄 Breaking Changes If this is a breaking change, please describe: **No, this is not a breaking change.** It is a bug fix that makes the existing Camera Test component function as designed. ## 📝 Additional Notes The original code attempted to set `videoRef.current.srcObject` immediately after calling `setStream(mediaStream)`. prs.title: Added longest common subsequences implementation in Python
prs.body: Implemented Longest Common Subsequence (LCS) algorithm in Python with memoization, tabulation, and space-optimized variants. Includes comprehensive documentation, test cases, performance benchmarks, and bonus feature to return actual LCS string.

prs.title: Feature/merge sort
prs.body: Implemented MergeSort algorithm in Python with simple and in-place versions, complete with documentation, test cases, and performance benchmarks. Matches the style and structure of the existing QuickSort implementation.

prs.title: Added house robber in python
prs.body: Refactored House Robber solution from top-down recursive approach to bottom-up iterative DP for improved readability and consistency with other DP solutions in the codebase. Added comprehensive documentation, inline comments, and test cases.

prs.title: Added: snake-game
prs.body: Added Snake Game implementation using Python's turtle module. Players control a snake to eat food, grow longer, and avoid hitting walls or their own body. Includes score tracking, progressive difficulty, and smooth keyboard controls.

prs.title: Added CSS artwork: Jellyfish by Chanakya
prs.body: Jellyfish - An immersive underwater CSS art featuring five glowing jellyfish with pulsing bells and flowing tentacles, accompanied by swimming fish, rising bubbles, swaying seaweed, and ethereal light rays filtering through the depths.

prs.title: Added mini mario game
prs.body: 🎮 Completed Super Platform Adventure – a full-featured Mario-style platformer built with HTML5, CSS3, and vanilla JavaScript. Includes smooth physics, coins, enemies, levels, scoring, and mobile controls. Ready for play and customization!

prs.title: Added Css artwork: Vintage Typewriter by Chanakya
prs.body: Added Vintage Typewriter CSS art featuring animated typing text, moving keys, striking type bar, rotating ribbon, and golden roller knobs. Created a fully responsive retro typewriter with mechanical details and smooth animations.

prs.title: Added expense tracker project
prs.body: Added a new Expense Tracker project to the Web Dev Mini Projects repo. Users can add, view, and delete expenses with a simple and responsive UI. Includes HTML, CSS, JS files, README, and a screenshot preview.
